title: Offer Legibility

content.question: If a customer lands on your business's homepage for 5 seconds, do they know the problem, outcome, and mechanism?
content.equation: OL = ((Problem Clarity + Outcome Clarity + Mechanism Clarity)/3) × Comprehension
sop: Write one sentence each for the problem you solve, the outcome you deliver, and the mechanism you use, no jargon allowed.
Show your homepage or pitch to 5 people outside your industry and time how long it takes them to explain it back correctly.
Rate Problem Clarity, Outcome Clarity, and Mechanism Clarity 1-10 based on that test (see calculation, page 5)
Cut every sentence that doesn't serve problem, outcome, or mechanism, aim for one clear line per section.
Re-test with 5 new people next week and track whether comprehension time drops.
content.explainer: People don't buy what they don't understand. Clarity is conversion.
questions: If a customer lands on your business's homepage for 5 seconds, do they know the problem, outcome, and mechanism?
Could a stranger explain your offer back to you correctly after reading it once?
Which of the three, problem, outcome, or mechanism, is the one your homepage explains worst right now?
content.equation_example: Rate Problem Clarity, Outcome Clarity, and Mechanism Clarity 1-10, then rate Comprehension 1-10 (how well testers explained your offer back). Problem Clarity 8, Outcome Clarity 7, Mechanism Clarity 6, average 7; Comprehension 6 → OL = 7 × 6 = 42 out of a possible 100. Higher OL means strangers get your offer in seconds; lower means confusion is costing you conversions before anyone even considers buying.
content.closing_statement: If they have to think, you've already lost them.

caption:
Principle Nº 6: Offer Legibility

Confusion kills conversion.

Ask yourself: If a customer lands on your business's homepage for 5 seconds, do they know the problem, outcome, and mechanism?
